research.interface: Terraform Enterprise REST API v2 (/api/v2, JSON:API): workspaces and state-versions endpoints (stable)

official_sources.title: API documentation for Terraform Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: /api/v2 base path, Authorization: Bearer token auth, application/vnd.api+json JSON:API conventions, JSON:API errors array shape, and 404 responses masking unauthorized resources

official_sources.title: /state-versions API reference for Terraform Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: GET /workspaces/:workspace_id/current-state-version and GET /state-versions/:state_version_id; hosted-state-download-url / hosted-json-state-download-url as temporary pre-signed archivist URLs; serial, size, status (pending/finalized/discarded), resources-processed; and the absence of md5/lineage in the show attributes (md5 is a create-request attribute only)

research.language_rationale: There is no official HashiCorp TypeScript SDK for the TFE API; the documented integration surface is the raw JSON:API REST contract at /api/v2, and Node's built-in fetch/http plus node:fs is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to implement a downloader that needs manual redirect control and atomic writes.

verified_facts: A workspace fetched by organization and name at GET /api/v2/organizations/:organization/workspaces/:name carries a current-state-version relationship whose data is a {type: state-versions, id} linkage, or null when no state exists.

GET /api/v2/state-versions/:state_version_id (and GET /api/v2/workspaces/:workspace_id/current-state-version) return attributes including hosted-state-download-url, hosted-json-state-download-url, serial, size, status, and resources-processed.
hosted-state-download-url is a temporary pre-signed archivist object URL (https://.../v1/object/<id> form) from which the raw state bytes are downloaded; it is not itself a JSON:API endpoint.

The state version show response has no md5 and no lineage attribute; md5 appears only in the create-state-version request body, so a downloader's integrity checks rest on the optional size attribute and on cross-checking the serial field inside the downloaded raw state JSON.
State version status values are pending, finalized, and discarded; serial increases every time Terraform writes new state, and the same serial appears inside the raw state document itself.

All /api/v2 requests carry Authorization: Bearer <token>; errors arrive as JSON:API error documents ({"errors": [{status, title, detail}]}), and 404 is returned both for missing resources and for resources the token is not authorized to see.
